channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mamelodi Police Arrest Two Suspects in Electricity Extortion Case
item.description: In Mamelodi, police have arrested two men accused of extorting households by demanding payments for illegal electricity connections. The operation began after complaints from residents in Selahliwe ab...

item.title: US confirms limited Nvidia H200 AI chip shipments to China amid tech restrictions
item.description: A US official revealed that a small number of Nvidia's H200 AI chips, one of the company's most powerful offerings, have been shipped to China. Jeffrey Kessler, under secretary of commerce for industr...

item.title: Politician faces backlash after alleged political retaliation for police misconduct allegations
item.description: The article details Moira Deeming's public dispute with the Victorian Liberal Party over their decision to distance themselves from her following a police complaint. Deeming claims the party is retali...

item.title: IEBC Warns Against Govt Officials Interfering in Ol Kalou Polling Stations
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has issued a stern warning to government officials and politicians against accessing polling stations during the Ol Kalou by-election. Commis...

item.title: Security forces intensify operations against terrorists in Balochistan during Operation Shaban
item.description: Operation Shaban, launched in retaliation for a deadly attack on a police post at the Mangi Dam pumping station in Ziarat, has resulted in the killing of 88 terrorists as security forces continue join...

item.title: AI Research Uncovers Complex Factors Behind Radicalization
item.description: Mijke van den Hurk, an AI scientist, investigated how artificial intelligence could help unravel the intricate factors driving radicalization. Her Ph.D. research, conducted in collaboration with the D...

item.title: Governor Lawal Distributes Agricultural Resources to Zamfara Farmers
item.description: Gov Dauda Lawal has distributed over 120 trucks of fertilizers and essential farm inputs to Zamfara state farmers as part of the 2026 wet-season fertilizer program. The initiative, launched at Gusau's...

item.title: AFN Chief Warns of Federal Projects Impact on First Nations Rights and Infrastructure
item.description: Cindy Woodhouse Nepinak, National Chief of the Assembly of First Nations (AFN), has raised significant concerns about the federal government's push to fast-track major infrastructure projects. Speakin...

item.title: British man's Iran jail sentence extended by two years, family says
item.description: A British man jailed in Iran after being accused of espionage has had two additional years added to his decade-long sentence, according to his family. Craig Foreman, along with his wife Lindsay, was a...

item.title: Tanzania's Rural Road Challenges Hinder Development
item.description: Tanzania continues to face significant challenges with rural road infrastructure despite progress on major highways. Poor conditions in district and village roads isolate remote communities from essen...

item.title: Pete Davidson's tattoo journey: Removal and new face tattoo
item.description: Pete Davidson, the comedian and actor, has been removing most of his tattoos since 2020, a decision influenced by his career in film and his recovery from addiction. He spent around $200,000 to remove...

item.title: Umaro Sissoco Embaló's Role in Macky Sall’s UN Campaign Highlights Political Loyalty
item.description: The article discusses the political alliance between former Guinea-Bissau President Umaro Sissoco Embaló and Senegalese President Macky Sall during his campaign for the United Nations secretary-genera...

item.title: SA Rugby Addresses Ticket Pricing Concerns Amid Sustainability Focus
item.description: SA Rugby president Mark Alexander has defended the organisation's decision to maintain high ticket prices for international matches, stating that it is a necessary measure for long-term sustainability...

item.title: Brumbies Finalize Triston Reilly Deal as New Squad Shaped
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are poised to announce Triston Reilly as their final recruit for the upcoming season, following his departure from the NSW Waratahs. The 27-year-old player, who has previously played ...

item.title: Spain Defeats France 2-0 to Reach FIFA World Cup Final
item.description: Spain secured a 2-0 semifinal victory over favored France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup, advancing to their second-ever final. Mikel Oyarzabal opened the scoring in the 22nd minute with a penalty, and Pe...

item.title: MTN empowers Nigerian youths through innovation and collaboration in Kano
item.description: MTN Nigeria has expanded its youth engagement initiative, The Gathering 100, to Kano, creating a platform for young innovators, entrepreneurs, and creatives to collaborate and showcase ideas. The even...

item.title: NITDA pushes for digital integration in Nigeria's infrastructure projects to boost economic growth
item.description: The National Information Technology Development Agency (NITDA) is advocating for the integration of digital components into all new infrastructure projects in Nigeria to support industrialization and ...

item.title: Pudukottai farmer leverages digital tools and eco-friendly methods for agricultural success
item.description: S. Muthulakshmi, a 50-year-old paddy farmer from Thirumalairayasamuthiram village in Pudukkottai District, has transformed her farming practices through training by the M.S. Swaminathan Research Found...

item.title: A mother's 17-year quest for justice after her murder in Crete
item.description: Jean Hanlon, a 53-year-old woman from Dumfries, Scotland, moved to Crete in 2003, where she built a new life. Her murder in 2009 remained unsolved for 17 years until her killer was finally convicted. ...

item.title: Criticism of a Hypothetical Question to Politicians
item.description: The article critiques the practice of journalists asking politicians to 'rule out' hypothetical scenarios, such as governing with One Nation, which the author deems a trivial and inane question. It re...
